title: python cleanup
body:
Description
---------------

`lib/core/dictionary.py` -> fix typo, remove redundant parenthesis since you are returning a tuple anyway
`lib/core/structures.py` -> remove redundant class parenthesis (not needed in python3)
`lib/reports/html_report.py` -> simplify comparison statements

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Apply small Python3 stylistic cleanups: fix a docstring typo and remove redundant parentheses in lib/core/dictionary.py, remove empty class parentheses in lib/core/structures.py, and simplify numeric range comparisons in lib/reports/html_report.py to use chained comparisons. All changes are syntactic and intended to be behavior-preserving.

steps.reason: Correct a small wording error in the generate() docstring ('no present' -> 'not present') to improve documentation clarity; no logic changes are required.
steps.side_effects: Documentation-only change. No behavioral impact on runtime, tests, or public API. No downstream code changes required.

steps.operation: Remove redundant parentheses from tuple return in __getstate__
steps.reason: Stylistic Python3 cleanup: change 'return (self._items, self._index)' to 'return self._items, self._index' since the comma already creates a tuple. This preserves behavior while making the code more idiomatic.
steps.side_effects: Behavior-preserving change. Unit tests and callers of __getstate__ should be unaffected. Also as part of this small Python3 cleanup PR, remove redundant empty class parentheses in lib/core/structures.py (change 'class OrderedSet():' to 'class OrderedSet:') — that is a separate, safe, behavior-preserving edit that should not affect tests or APIs.

steps.operation: Simplify numeric range comparisons in HTMLReport.generate
steps.reason: Replace verbose boolean expressions like 'entry.status >= 200 and entry.status <= 299' with idiomatic chained comparisons '200 <= entry.status <= 299' (and analogous ranges for 300-399 and 400-599). This improves readability without changing behavior.
steps.side_effects: Behavior-preserving syntax-only change. No change to output or API. Existing tests that inspect HTMLReport output should pass unchanged; if any tests assert exact source text of the function, they will need updating, but this is unlikely.
